criticality: "high"

---

# UC-20.3.10 ¬∑ ServiceNow Fulfiller versus Requester License Mix

## Description

Detects ServiceNow users who hold one or more Fulfiller-tier roles (`itil`, `change_manager`, `incident_manager`, `agent_workspace_user`, `sn_customerservice_agent`, etc.) yet have either never logged in or have not logged in within the 365-day audit window ‚Äî the population that ServiceNow's billing engine still counts as a paid Fulfiller seat regardless of activity. Layers an over-assignment skew check (Fulfiller-role holders versus active Requester ticket-openers) so over-provisioning is visible even when individual revocation candidates are spread thin.

## Value

Fulfiller seats list at $70‚Äì220/user/month (Standard through Enterprise tier) and ServiceNow's compliance count is driven by role assignment, not by activity, so every dormant `itil` role is real money on the next renewal. A 1,000-fulfiller estate carrying the typical 15‚Äì25% over-assignment is $216K‚Äì$432K of pure shelfware per year (worse at Enterprise tier and worse with the standard 7‚Äì9% annual uplift). This UC gives the ITAM/SAM team an evidenced, time-stamped revocation list plus a fulfiller-to-requester ratio metric the ServiceNow account team cannot dispute, in time to act before the audit window closes at renewal.

## Implementation

Install `Splunk_TA_snow` on a Heavy Forwarder and on every Search Head; add a ServiceNow OAuth account with `display_value=all`. Enable the built-in `sys_user`, `incident`, `change_request`, and `sc_request` inputs. Add a CUSTOM modular input for `sys_user_has_role` (the add-on does not collect this table by default) plus a weekly snapshot stanza. Run the alert on Monday mornings, route to the ITAM/SAM distribution list, and feed actioned revocations back to ServiceNow ‚Üí User Administration. Reconcile `fulfiller_total` against ServiceNow's License Compliance Manager every quarter and again 9‚Äì12 months before contract renewal.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (`Splunk_TA_snow`, [Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)) ‚â•7.x installed on a Heavy Forwarder (modular inputs do not run on a Universal Forwarder) and on every Search Head that needs the field aliases / sourcetype-level extractions.
‚Ä¢ A dedicated ServiceNow integration account. Required table read ACLs: `sys_user`, `sys_user_has_role`, `sys_user_role`, `sys_user_group`, `sys_user_grmember`, `sys_group_has_role`, `incident`, `change_request`, `sc_request`. Best practice: create the account with the OOB `snc_internal` role plus explicit table read ACLs ‚Äî do not use `admin`. Set `web_service_access_only=true` on this account so it does not appear in its own search results.
‚Ä¢ Index: this UC assumes `index=itsm`. If you use a different index, replace `index=itsm` everywhere in the SPL and update `srchIndexesAllowed` on the consuming role.
‚Ä¢ Splunk role: users running this search need `srchIndexesAllowed = itsm`. Create a custom role (`itam_observer`) rather than granting `admin`.
‚Ä¢ ServiceNow Now Platform release: tested on Tokyo / Utah / Vancouver / Washington / Yokohama. The `sys_user_has_role` table and the `last_login_time` field on `sys_user` exist in every release back to Geneva.
‚Ä¢ A `servicenow_licence_tier_cost.csv` lookup with two columns (`tier`, `monthly_cost_usd`). Default values that match the public Fulfiller-versus-Requester benchmark mid-points: `Standard,85`, `Pro,108`, `Pro Plus,155`, `Enterprise,200`. Update with your negotiated rate after the next renewal.
‚Ä¢ Knowledge: a current export from your ServiceNow License Compliance Manager (LCM) used in Step 3 reconciliation. If you do not yet have access to LCM, ServiceNow's Subscription Management module exposes the same numbers under System Definition ‚Üí Subscription Management.

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Install the add-on on the Heavy Forwarder and Search Heads:

```
splunk install app /tmp/splunk-add-on-for-servicenow_*.tgz
splunk restart
```

In Splunk Web ‚Üí Apps ‚Üí Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow ‚Üí Configuration:
‚Ä¢ Add a ServiceNow account. Auth options are Basic, OAuth (instance grant), and OAuth (JWT). Prefer OAuth for production; the password-based Basic option is deprecated by ServiceNow for new instances. Account name (e.g. `prod_snow`) is referenced by every input stanza below.
‚Ä¢ Set Display values to `all` on every input that involves reference fields. Without this, fields like `user` and `role` in `sys_user_has_role` come back as 32-character `sys_id` hex strings instead of readable login names and role labels, and the SPL's `match(lower(role),...)` filter returns zero rows.

Enable the built-in table inputs that this UC consumes:
‚Ä¢ `sys_user` ‚Äî defaults to incremental polling on `sys_updated_on`. Add a secondary stanza that does a full nightly snapshot so dormant users (whose `sys_updated_on` has not moved in months) reappear in the index every night. Without this, you cannot reliably compute `days_since_last_login` for users who have not been touched recently.
‚Ä¢ `incident`, `change_request`, `sc_request` ‚Äî incremental polling is fine; this UC only needs 30 days of activity for the requester denominator.

Add the CUSTOM input for `sys_user_has_role` (the add-on does NOT collect this table by default). Edit `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_snow/local/inputs.conf` on the Heavy Forwarder:

```ini
[servicenow://sys_user_has_role_delta]
disabled = 0
account = prod_snow
duration = 86400
sourcetype = snow:sys_user_has_role
table = sys_user_has_role
include_table_info = 0
display_value = all
filter_data = ORDERBYsys_updated_on
index = itsm

[servicenow://sys_user_has_role_snapshot]
disabled = 0
account = prod_snow
duration = 604800
sourcetype = snow:sys_user_has_role
table = sys_user_has_role
include_table_info = 0
display_value = all
since_when = 0
index = itsm
```

The first stanza is the incremental delta (every 24h, picks up new and changed mappings). The second is a weekly full reload so the SPL's 90-day dedup window always finds at least one event per active mapping ‚Äî without it, role mappings that have not been edited recently age out of the index. Reload the HF (`splunk restart`) and verify ingestion: `index=itsm sourcetype=snow:sys_user_has_role | head 1 | table user role granted_by`. Three readable values, not sys_ids ‚Äî that's `display_value=all` working.

Verification of the `sys_user` snapshot: `index=itsm sourcetype=snow:sys_user earliest=-30d | stats dc(user_name) as users_seen` should be ‚â• your active employee headcount. If it equals only the count of users updated in the last 30 days, the snapshot stanza is not running.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Primary SPL (raw events, copy-paste-runnable):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:sys_user_has_role" earliest=-90d@d
| dedup user role sortby -_time
| eval is_fulfiller=if(match(lower(role),"^(itil|change_manager|incident_manager|problem_manager|task_writer|agent_workspace_user|sn_change_write|sn_incident_write|sn_customerservice_agent|admin)$"),1,0)
| stats max(is_fulfiller) as fulfiller_role values(role) as roles_held by user
| where fulfiller_role=1
| join type=inner user max=0 [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:sys_user" earliest=-7d@d
    | dedup user_name sortby -_time
    | rename user_name as user
    | where active="true" AND web_service_access_only!="true"
    | eval days_since_last_login=if(isnull(last_login_time) OR last_login_time="",-1,
                                    round((now()-strptime(last_login_time,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))/86400,0))
    | table user department days_since_last_login
  ]
| eval revocation_class=case(
    days_since_last_login=-1,  "01_never_logged_in",
    days_since_last_login>365, "02_audit_window_exceeded",
    days_since_last_login>180, "03_dormant_180d_revoke",
    days_since_last_login>90,  "04_dormant_90d_review",
    true(),                    "05_active_paid")
| eventstats count as fulfiller_total
| appendcols [
    search index=itsm (sourcetype="snow:incident" OR sourcetype="snow:sc_request") earliest=-30d@d
    | stats dc(caller_id) as active_requesters_30d
  ]
| filldown active_requesters_30d
| eval fulfiller_to_requester_ratio_pct=round(100*fulfiller_total/coalesce(active_requesters_30d,1),1)
| stats count as users by revocation_class, fulfiller_total, active_requesters_30d, fulfiller_to_requester_ratio_pct
| eval annual_waste_estimate_usd=if(revocation_class IN ("01_never_logged_in","02_audit_window_exceeded","03_dormant_180d_revoke"),users*120*12,0)
| sort revocation_class
```

How the pipeline maps to the ServiceNow compliance count:

‚Ä¢ The first stage scopes the role-mapping table over a 90-day window (covering the weekly snapshot polling period from Step 1) and dedups so each `(user, role)` pair contributes once at its latest seen state.
‚Ä¢ The Fulfiller-tier role list mirrors the canonical ServiceNow Fulfiller licence definition: `itil` for ITSM Standard/Pro; `change_manager`, `incident_manager`, `problem_manager`, `task_writer`, `agent_workspace_user` for the operational ITSM workflow; `sn_change_write` and `sn_incident_write` for the Pro Plus / Now Assist scoped writers; `sn_customerservice_agent` for CSM; and `admin` (anyone with `admin` is a Fulfiller by definition). Add or remove role names to match your contract ‚Äî modules like HRSD (`sn_hr_core_*`), SecOps (`sn_si_*`), and any custom paid scoped apps (`x_<vendor_prefix>_*`) need their own entries in the regex.
‚Ä¢ The inner join with `snow:sys_user` filters out the two populations ServiceNow's own compliance engine excludes from the Fulfiller count: inactive users (`active!="true"`) and integration accounts (`web_service_access_only="true"`). This is how you reconcile to ServiceNow's License Compliance Manager number in Step 3.
‚Ä¢ `case(days_since_last_login...)` buckets users into the four operational classes used by the ITAM workflow:
  ‚Äì `01_never_logged_in`: provisioning leftover, almost always safe to revoke on confirmation that the user has departed or that the role was assigned in error.
  ‚Äì `02_audit_window_exceeded`: past the 365-day window ServiceNow uses in some contracts for compliance counts. The role is still assigned and the line item is still on the bill.
  ‚Äì `03_dormant_180d_revoke`: revocation candidate after manager confirmation.
  ‚Äì `04_dormant_90d_review`: light user, often a candidate for downgrade from Fulfiller to Approver tier (saves 30‚Äì50% per seat per the public benchmarks).
‚Ä¢ The `appendcols` block computes the active-Requester denominator from 30 days of inbound tickets. ServiceNow billing is driven by the ratio of operational seats to actual business activity, not by absolute Fulfiller count alone. A healthy ratio sits around 10‚Äì20% Fulfillers; >35% is the over-assignment signal called out in the public Fulfiller-versus-Requester benchmarks as a renewal-true-up risk.
‚Ä¢ `annual_waste_estimate_usd` defaults to $120/user/month (mid-Pro tier per public benchmarks). For a tier-aware estimate, replace the constant `120` with `lookup servicenow_licence_tier_cost.csv tier OUTPUT monthly_cost_usd` joined on each user's contracted tier.

Schedule as Alert: cron `0 6 * * 1` (Monday 06:00 ‚Äî runs after the weekend snapshot poll), time range `-90d@d to now`, trigger on "Number of results > 0 where revocation_class IN ('01_never_logged_in','02_audit_window_exceeded','03_dormant_180d_revoke') AND users > 0". Throttle by `revocation_class` for 7 days so the same bucket does not re-fire weekly while remediation is in flight. Notify the ITAM / SAM distribution list, not the on-call SRE ‚Äî this is a financial and audit signal, not an outage.

Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
(a) Reconcile against ServiceNow's own License Compliance Manager (LCM): in ServiceNow ‚Üí System Definition ‚Üí License Compliance Manager ‚Üí ITSM (or Subscription Management ‚Üí Allocation depending on your edition). The LCM's Fulfiller count for ITSM should agree with `fulfiller_total` from the SPL within ¬±2%. The small drift comes from LCM-only fields like `licensable_user=false` overrides which are not exposed on `sys_user` ‚Äî if the gap is >5%, your role-list regex is incomplete (Step 2) or your `web_service_access_only` flag is set inconsistently across integration accounts (Step 5 troubleshooting).

(b) Spot-check a known dormant user. Pick someone whose ITSM role you know is unused ‚Äî a contractor whose engagement ended ‚â•6 months ago. `index=itsm sourcetype="snow:sys_user" user_name="<that-user>" earliest=-30d | head 1 | table active last_login_time web_service_access_only`. `active="true"`, `last_login_time` six months old, `web_service_access_only="false"` ‚Üí the user must appear in `02_audit_window_exceeded` or `03_dormant_180d_revoke` after the next alert run.

(c) Confirm the Fulfiller role list is complete for your contract. Run `index=itsm sourcetype="snow:sys_user_has_role" earliest=-90d@d | stats dc(user) as users_with_role by role | sort -users_with_role | head 50`. Cross-reference each role in the top 50 against your contract's Fulfiller-tier role list. Add any missing roles to the regex in Step 2 ‚Äî particularly the `sn_*` writer roles for Pro Plus, the `sn_csm_*` roles for CSM, and any `x_<scope>_admin` roles introduced by custom paid scoped apps.

(d) Confirm the inverse ‚Äî that `active_requesters_30d` is non-trivial. If it's zero or near-zero, either `caller_id` is not being populated by your portal (some Service Portal configs use `requested_for` instead), or the index/sourcetype scope is wrong. Verify with `index=itsm sourcetype=snow:incident earliest=-30d | stats dc(caller_id) dc(opened_by) dc(requested_for)`. Use whichever distinct count is highest in the SPL's `appendcols` subsearch.

(e) Confirm Splunk role permissions: `| rest splunk_server=local /servicesNS/-/-/authorization/roles | search title=itam_observer | table title srchIndexesAllowed`. The list must include `itsm`.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Dashboard layout (recommended, name it "ServiceNow Fulfiller Licence Mix ‚Äî Right-Sizing"):
‚Ä¢ Row 1: Single-value tile pair ‚Äî "Paid Fulfiller users (active, billable)" and "Annual waste estimate (USD, dormant + audit-window-exceeded buckets)". Red threshold ‚â• $250K. Sub-text: link to the ITAM runbook.
‚Ä¢ Row 2: Bar chart of `users` split by `revocation_class`, labelled with the operational action for each bucket. Click-through opens the per-user detail table.
‚Ä¢ Row 3: Sortable per-user table ‚Äî `user`, `department`, `roles_held`, `days_since_last_login`, `last_active_record_date` (joined from `snow:incident` / `snow:change_request` / `snow:sys_audit` on `assigned_to=user OR opened_by=user`). Drilldown opens the user's profile in ServiceNow via the `sys_id` URL.
‚Ä¢ Row 4: 12-month timechart of fulfiller-count by month, split by `department`. Useful at renewal-prep time to surface M&A integration drift and post-reorg accumulation.
‚Ä¢ Row 5: Single-value `fulfiller_to_requester_ratio_pct` with traffic-light thresholds: green <20%, amber 20‚Äì35%, red >35%.

Alerting:
‚Ä¢ Email to the ITAM / SAM distribution list every Monday morning. Weekly is the right cadence ‚Äî daily creates noise, monthly misses incident-driven changes from M&A activity, reorgs, and project-team rotations.
‚Ä¢ Annotate the email with the saved-search PNG of Row 2 so the recipient sees the bucket distribution without opening Splunk.
‚Ä¢ Slack/Teams secondary notification to `#itam-snow` for visibility, no paging.

Runbook (owner: ITAM / SAM team lead):
1. Open the per-user detail table for `revocation_class IN ("01_never_logged_in","02_audit_window_exceeded")`.
2. For each user, confirm employment status via the HR system feed (Workday, SuccessFactors, or BambooHR). Departed ‚Üí revoke immediately in ServiceNow ‚Üí User Administration ‚Üí Users ‚Üí <user> ‚Üí "Revoke roles".
3. For active employees in those two buckets: e-mail the user's manager from the saved-search action with a 14-day "speak now or we revoke" notice, citing the user's `last_login_time`.
4. After 14 days: revoke the role via ServiceNow ‚Üí User Administration ‚Üí Users ‚Üí <user> ‚Üí Roles ‚Üí Edit. The user's account stays active (so they remain a free Requester) but the Fulfiller billing line drops at the next compliance recount.
5. For `03_dormant_180d_revoke`: same workflow, but manager confirmation strongly recommended before revoking ‚Äî these are people who were active 6 months ago.
6. For `04_dormant_90d_review`: review whether the user should be downgraded from Fulfiller to Approver tier. Approver licences cost 30‚Äì50% less than Fulfiller licences and grant approval authority + dashboard view without record-management capabilities ‚Äî sufficient for managers and CAB members who only approve.

Capacity review (cadence: quarterly, owner: ITAM):
‚Ä¢ Compute revocations actioned versus revocations identified per quarter; target ‚â•80% action rate so the over-assignment does not re-accumulate.
‚Ä¢ Update the `servicenow_licence_tier_cost.csv` lookup with the actual contracted rate from the most-recent renewal; also update the row tier per user from the LCM allocation export so the `annual_waste_estimate_usd` reflects what each user actually costs.
‚Ä¢ 9‚Äì12 months before the contract renewal end-date, snapshot the dashboard for renewal preparation. The fulfiller-count timechart (Row 4) plus the ratio metric (Row 5) become inputs into the negotiation alongside the LCM's own usage report.

Step 5 ‚Äî Troubleshooting
‚Ä¢ No `snow:sys_user_has_role` events at all ‚Äî the custom input from Step 1 was not added or was not reloaded. On the Heavy Forwarder: `splunk btool inputs list --debug | grep sys_user_has_role`. Confirm `disabled = 0` from your local app, not `disabled = 1` from `Splunk_TA_snow/default/`. Tail `$SPLUNK_HOME/var/log/splunk/splunkd.log` and the add-on's own log (`$SPLUNK_HOME/var/log/splunk/ta_servicenow_*.log`) for `sys_user_has_role` mentions and any HTTP 401 / 403 entries. Common cause: the integration account does not have read ACL on the `sys_user_has_role` table ‚Äî fix in ServiceNow ‚Üí System Security ‚Üí Access Control (ACL) ‚Üí New, table=`sys_user_has_role`, operation=read, role=`snc_internal`.

‚Ä¢ `role` and `user` come back as 32-character hex (sys_ids) ‚Äî `display_value` is not set to `all` on the input stanza. Edit the stanza, restart the Heavy Forwarder, and the next poll pulls human-readable values. The historical sys_id-only events stay sys_id-only ‚Äî re-run the snapshot stanza with `since_when = 0` or wait one week for the snapshot stanza to fire to backfill.

‚Ä¢ `fulfiller_total` is materially lower than ServiceNow's own License Compliance Manager number ‚Äî your fulfiller-role regex is incomplete. Run validation step (c) and add the missing roles. Pay particular attention to module-specific roles: `sn_csm_*` for CSM, `sn_hr_core_*` for HRSD, `sn_si_*` for SecOps, and custom roles starting with `x_<vendor_prefix>_*` for paid scoped apps from the ServiceNow Store.

‚Ä¢ `days_since_last_login` is `-1` (never logged in) for users you know are active ‚Äî the user is using SSO and the SSO redirect-to-app pattern does not update `last_login_time`. ServiceNow has a known issue here when `glide.authenticate.user.password.change_after_login=false` and the SSO IdP issues long-lived tokens. Workaround: derive last-active from `assigned_to` records: `index=itsm (sourcetype="snow:incident" OR sourcetype="snow:change_request") earliest=-180d | stats max(_time) as last_assigned_record_time by assigned_to`. Use `last_assigned_record_time` instead of `last_login_time` for these users. This is also the recommended approach for any organisation that runs ServiceNow behind a SAML or OIDC SSO with stayed-signed-in tokens.

‚Ä¢ High false-positive rate on integration / service accounts ‚Äî the pattern is users with `web_service_access_only="false"` who are in fact integration accounts. Add an exclusion: in the inner join, `| where NOT match(lower(user_name),"^(svc|sa|sn-|api-|integration|service)")` OR maintain a `servicenow_service_account_allowlist.csv` lookup. Also push the fix upstream ‚Äî the provisioning team should be setting `web_service_access_only=true` at account creation; a corrective bulk update via the UI Action "Mark as web service account" closes the gap for historical accounts.

‚Ä¢ Rows for `01_never_logged_in` keep growing week over week ‚Äî your provisioning pipeline is granting Fulfiller roles before the user logs in for the first time. Either delay role assignment until first login (a workflow change in ServiceNow ‚Üí System Definition ‚Üí Workflow), or treat new users (`sys_created_on > now()-14d`) as a separate `06_pending_first_login` bucket that does not roll into `annual_waste_estimate_usd`.

‚Ä¢ `active_requesters_30d` is implausibly low ‚Äî `caller_id` may not be the right field for your portal. Switch to `requested_for` for `snow:sc_request` events: replace the `appendcols` subsearch with `[ search index=itsm (sourcetype="snow:incident" caller_id=*) OR (sourcetype="snow:sc_request" requested_for=*) earliest=-30d | eval requester=coalesce(caller_id, requested_for) | stats dc(requester) as active_requesters_30d ]`. Also check whether anonymous Service Portal sessions set `caller_id=guest` ‚Äî exclude with `caller_id!="guest"`.

‚Ä¢ Splunk schedules the alert but it consistently times out ‚Äî the `join` on `user` against the `sys_user` snapshot scales with user-count. For estates >50K active users, switch to a lookup-driven approach: a nightly saved search builds `servicenow_user_roles_snapshot.csv` with columns `user, fulfiller_role, days_since_last_login, department`. The alert SPL becomes `| inputlookup servicenow_user_roles_snapshot.csv | where fulfiller_role=1 | eval revocation_class=case(...) | ...`. This shifts the join from search-time to a one-shot nightly build and brings runtime from minutes to seconds.

‚Ä¢ LCM disagrees by exactly the count of one department ‚Äî that department is mid-reorg and ServiceNow has been re-keying users between departments while the SPL is reading a stale snapshot from the `sys_user` incremental input. Force a full snapshot refresh of `sys_user` (set `since_when = 0` on the snapshot stanza, restart the HF), wait one full polling cycle, then re-run the SPL.

## SPL

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:sys_user_has_role" earliest=-90d@d
| dedup user role sortby -_time
| eval is_fulfiller=if(match(lower(role),"^(itil|change_manager|incident_manager|problem_manager|task_writer|agent_workspace_user|sn_change_write|sn_incident_write|sn_customerservice_agent|admin)$"),1,0)
| stats max(is_fulfiller) as fulfiller_role values(role) as roles_held by user
| where fulfiller_role=1
| join type=inner user max=0 [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:sys_user" earliest=-7d@d
    | dedup user_name sortby -_time
    | rename user_name as user
    | where active="true" AND web_service_access_only!="true"
    | eval days_since_last_login=if(isnull(last_login_time) OR last_login_time="",-1,
                                    round((now()-strptime(last_login_time,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))/86400,0))
    | table user department days_since_last_login
  ]
| eval revocation_class=case(
    days_since_last_login=-1,  "01_never_logged_in",
    days_since_last_login>365, "02_audit_window_exceeded",
    days_since_last_login>180, "03_dormant_180d_revoke",
    days_since_last_login>90,  "04_dormant_90d_review",
    true(),                    "05_active_paid")
| eventstats count as fulfiller_total
| appendcols [
    search index=itsm (sourcetype="snow:incident" OR sourcetype="snow:sc_request") earliest=-30d@d
    | stats dc(caller_id) as active_requesters_30d
  ]
| filldown active_requesters_30d
| eval fulfiller_to_requester_ratio_pct=round(100*fulfiller_total/coalesce(active_requesters_30d,1),1)
| stats count as users by revocation_class, fulfiller_total, active_requesters_30d, fulfiller_to_requester_ratio_pct
| eval annual_waste_estimate_usd=if(revocation_class IN ("01_never_logged_in","02_audit_window_exceeded","03_dormant_180d_revoke"),users*120*12,0)
| sort revocation_class
```

## Visualization

(1) Single-value tile pair: "Paid Fulfiller users (active, billable)" and "Annual waste estimate (USD, dormant + audit-window-exceeded buckets)" ‚Äî red threshold ‚â• $250K. (2) Bar chart of `users` split by `revocation_class`, labelled with the operational action for each bucket (revoke immediately / manager confirm / downgrade to Approver). (3) Sortable per-user table: `user`, `department`, `roles_held`, `days_since_last_login`, `last_active_record_date` (joined from `snow:incident`/`snow:change_request` on `assigned_to=user`); row click drilldowns to the user's profile in ServiceNow. (4) 12-month timechart of fulfiller-count split by `department` ‚Äî surfaces M&A integration drift and post-reorg accumulation in time for renewal prep. (5) Single-value `fulfiller_to_requester_ratio_pct` with traffic-light thresholds (green <20%, amber 20‚Äì35%, red >35%) per the public Fulfiller-versus-Requester benchmark range.
